localizations.title: Around the World in 80 minutes
localizations.hint_0: Find your next destination in the travel diary, so you will have a better idea of what time it is.
localizations.hint_2: Placed at the right spot, this banana peel could save your day.
localizations.hint_3: The banana (3) results from the blue (the banana) + red (the peel) combination. To remove the peel from the banana, there is only one possible combination of numbers.
localizations.hint_7: £55,000! That will finance the whole trip!
localizations.hint_8: Generally speaking, why are passports visaed?
localizations.hint_9: The men who are chasing you are on very steep terrain. It wouldn't take much to make them all fall down.
localizations.hint_10_first: Once you place the card appropriately, you can climb up a tree and try to spot the guide.
localizations.hint_10: Place card 10 above card 11. What do you see?
localizations.hint_11: Your fall caused some memory loss. How did you get here? Take the travel diary to find the starting point of your adventure.
localizations.hint_13: As soon as you are abroad, this visa will be proof of your travel.
localizations.hint_14: To avoid hurting yourself on the nails, you must always divide your weight on at least 3 supports.
localizations.hint_16: Despite the language barrier, you should be able to pick up something in what this man is telling you.
localizations.hint_18: This elephant is very stubborn and does not follow the main road, but it has other qualities. Did you know that an elephant's trunk is like a Swiss Army knife? It is not only used to breathe, but also to trumpet, drink, sprinkle water or dust, scratch...
localizations.hint_21: How many days have passed since your departure?
localizations.hint_30: Now that you are barefoot, you shouldn't be bothered anymore.
localizations.hint_34_first: Each of the four stops of your journey will provide you with a piece of the telegram to send (dot or dash).
localizations.hint_34: Observe the shape of the 4 visas you have collected so far.
localizations.hint_37: You feel someone is observing you…
localizations.hint_42_first: Who could have stolen the keys from this unfortunate servant?
localizations.hint_42: When you look more closely, you notice a few monkeys. Could it be that one of them had something to do with this fuss about keys?
localizations.hint_43: These are the keys to the gate.
localizations.hint_46: You have something this man cannot resist.
localizations.hint_48: You first must remember everything that happened until Passepartout fell. Then decide, using this compass, which direction you should head in.
localizations.hint_54: If needs be, this water could save a life.
localizations.hint_56: If you remove his shoes, what's left of Passepartout?
localizations.hint_58: You saw an ad about a watch. Remember to bring the change back to Mr. Fogg.
localizations.hint_61: To travel the world, this ship must connect New York to your starting point: London. But how can you transcribe this in your diary?
localizations.hint_62: Carefully observe the ship that is docked.
localizations.hint_64: They do not accept English Pounds here. First, you must exchange your money.
localizations.hint_66: You have undeniable proof of your trip.
localizations.hint_69: Observe the shape the lights draw once at full speed.
localizations.hint_70: If you remove his shoes, what's left of Passepartout?
localizations.hint_72_first: Carefully observe the shape of the flames and find a way to transport the water from the Brahma waterfalls using the elephant.
localizations.hint_72: The flames form 40. Carefully observe the picture depicting the waterfalls.
localizations.hint_79: Once all the wood from the ship has been burned in the boiler, what's left of the ship?
localizations.hint_80: Your fall stuns you. You see double.
localizations.hint_81: Your watch indicates that you left London 81 days ago. However, this evening Hamlet is playing for the last time.
localizations.hint_85_first: You must unballast the balloon to take off.
localizations.hint_85: Remove the ballasts from the card number to take off. What is the new machine number?
localizations.hint_90: Once all the wood from the ship has been burned in the boiler, what's left of the ship?
localizations.hint_94: It was at that very moment when the guide was explaining his plan to Passepartout that the latter fell and banged his head. How did you manage to fall?
localizations.hint_96_first: Arrange the events in sequence. Use the backs of the cards to retrace your route from Hong-Kong.
localizations.hint_96: First, place the back of card 97 on the diary so to connect the route starting point to Hong-Kong. Make sure all the while to orientate your compass to the North. Then, after card 97, place cards 98 and 96, one after the other, still orientating to the North.
localizations.hint_97_first: Arrange the events in sequence. Use the backs of the cards to retrace your route from Hong-Kong.
localizations.hint_97: First, place the back of card 97 on the diary so to connect the route starting point to Hong-Kong. Make sure all the while to orientate your compass to the North. Then, after card 97, place cards 98 and 96, one after the other, still orientating to the North.
localizations.hint_98_first: Arrange the events in sequence. Use the backs of the cards to retrace your route from Hong-Kong.
localizations.hint_98: First, place the back of card 97 on the diary so to connect the route starting point to Hong-Kong. Make sure all the while to orientate your compass to the North. Then, after card 97, place cards 98 and 96, one after the other, still orientating to the North.
localizations.hint_100: Remove the Ka sounds and assemble the remaining sounds to form a number: SE (ka) VEN (ka) TY (ka) TWO (ka).
localizations.hint_111: Once the deck of cards is cut, place the lower pile on top of the other. The D on the edge will transform into another letter.
localizations.hint_122_first: Each player seems to betray his hand, one has an Ace, the other a Queen, and the third a Jack. As to your card, it is in front of you.
localizations.hint_122: You are Fogg and have a King. The other players betray their hands by mentioning them in thfeir dialogue. To your left, Jack has a Queen. To your right, Flanagan has a Jack. Across from you is Fallentin, your partner who has an Ace.
localizations.hint_133_first: The elephant did as it pleased and following its own route made you discover locations you would have never seen otherwise. You liked some of these locations so much that you decided to immortalize your visit.
localizations.hint_133: Use the photographs from the travel diary to retrace your route.
localizations.hint_144: You have Pounds and, here, in America you need Dollars. If you read the correct row in the correct way, you will know how to exchange your money.
localizations.hint_155: In which direction must you walk to reach the smoke and join your guide? The sunset gives you a landmark.
localizations.hint_200: Pay close attention to the rhythm of the message the lighthouse is sending you.
localizations.hint_300: Have a good look at the crowd on the left, next to the stalls...
localizations.answer_hint_0: Thanks to your diary, you know that Suez is your next destination. The train leaves at 8:45 pm, in 5 minutes according to Monsieur Fogg. So, it is 8:40 pm. Take this opportunity to set your watch to 8:40.
localizations.answer_hint_2: Throw this banana peel (2) at the feet of the men chasing you (9) to make them fall.
localizations.answer_hint_3: The only way to obtain 3 combining a blue and red number is by adding 2+1.
localizations.answer_hint_8: Give your passport  (13) to the Consul (8) so that he can visa it.
localizations.answer_hint_10: Place this card above card 11 to complete the vegetation.  A Y-shaped tree seems to rise high toward the canopy.
localizations.answer_hint_11: Read the first page of the diary (Day 1) to find the hidden number 4 in the text.
localizations.answer_hint_14: To start, simultaneously press the 4 supports. Each finger is associated with a color. Then, spot the color of the next support and only move the finger corresponding to that color. Repeat these steps until you have crossed the plank.
localizations.answer_hint_16: Number 56 is hidden in the man's statement.
localizations.answer_hint_18: If needs be, take some water from the Brahma waterfalls (hidden blue +36) using the elephant's trunk (18).
localizations.answer_hint_21: According to the newspaper, you left London (Day 1) on October 2, 1872. Thanks to your diary, you know that you visited Suez on Day 8, i.e.; 7 days after your departure. So, the date of your visit to the consulate is October 9, 1972. Set the stamp to that date.
localizations.answer_hint_30: You took off your shoes (30) as instructed by the man (16). Now, try to enter the sacred temple.
localizations.answer_hint_34: The shape of the visas produces either a dot or a dash. In the order of your route: Suez (dot), Bombay (dash), Calcutta (dash), and Kong-Kong (dash). Enter Dot/Dash/Dash/Dash in the telegraph. Thus, you find out which card to take next.
localizations.answer_hint_42: Attract the monkeys (42) with a banana (1).
localizations.answer_hint_43: Give the keys (43) to the servant (42) so that he gives you access to the hot-air balloon.
localizations.answer_hint_46: Try to convince this mahout (46) with a bribe (7).
localizations.answer_hint_56: Passepartout's shoe size is 40. Subtract 40 from Passepartout (70-40=30).
localizations.answer_hint_58: Mr. Fogg gave you £100. The watch from the newspaper is worth £73. After you bought it, you are left with £27 change.
localizations.answer_hint_61: Fold both sides of the map of the world toward the back to connect both extremities of the map. Then, place the ship (61) on the right spot to have number 81 appear.
localizations.answer_hint_62: Spot the two flags hanging from the mast of the ship. According to the maritime code in your diary, they correspond to the numbers 1 and 4.
localizations.answer_hint_64: To go from Pounds (£) to Dollars ($), 7 becomes 5 (2nd row). To pay the teller at the San Francisco train station, add 5 ($) and not 7 (£).
localizations.answer_hint_66: Show your passport (13) to the captain (66) to demonstrate your good faith.
localizations.answer_hint_69: The lights form the letter F.
localizations.answer_hint_70: Passepartout's shoe size is 40. Subtract 40 from Passepartout (70 - 40 = 30).
localizations.answer_hint_72: Once the water is taken by the elephant (hidden +36 + 18 = 54), throw it on the flames (40) to extinguish the fire and rescue the young woman.
localizations.answer_hint_79: According to the blueprint (79), the wood, all in all, weighs 29 tons out of the total 90-ton steamer. Once you burn all the wood, only the metal framework remains: 61 tons (90 - 29).
localizations.answer_hint_80: Passepartout has double vision, so the speech bubbles 33 and 99 are actually double images of 3 and 9.
localizations.answer_hint_81: Your watch displays Sunday, December 22nd; 81 days since you left London. However, according to the newspaper, Hamlet should have been performed for the last time on Saturday, December 21st. Is it possible that you only traveled around the world in 80 days? Set your watch backward 24 hours to find out...
localizations.answer_hint_85: To take off, you must remove 70 pounds from the hot-air balloon. Enter machine 15 (85 - 70) in the app.
localizations.answer_hint_90: According to the blueprint (79), the wood, all in all, weighs 29 tons out of the total 90-ton steamer. Once you burn all the wood, only the metal framework remains: 61 tons (90 - 29).
localizations.answer_hint_94: The rock that is the cause for Passepartout's fall has the hidden number 37.
localizations.answer_hint_96: Once you correctly placed the 3 cards on the diary, the dotted line leads to the letter C of Pacific.
localizations.answer_hint_97: Once you correctly placed the 3 cards on the diary, the dotted line leads to the letter C of Pacific.
localizations.answer_hint_98: Once you correctly placed the 3 cards on the diary, the dotted line leads to the letter C of Pacific.
localizations.answer_hint_100: SE VEN TY TWO. Take card 72.
localizations.answer_hint_111: When the bottom of the deck is on top, the edge forms the letter K.
localizations.answer_hint_122: Fallentin wins the 13th trick thanks to its Ace. Team 1 totals 5 and Team 2 totals 8.
localizations.answer_hint_133: When you look at the photographs in sequential order, you go from the Shamanic village to the Vishnu cave, then to the central camp, the Village of Ganesh, and finally the Brahma waterfalls. This route forms the letter M.
localizations.answer_hint_144: To go from Pounds (£) to Dollars ($), 7 becomes 5 (2nd row). To pay the teller at the San Francisco train station, add 5 ($) and not 7 (£).
localizations.answer_hint_155: The sun is setting (Y) and indicates West. If you use the compass (48), you can deduce that the smoke signal is to the North-West. Orientate the compass (48) to the North-West, then press OK.
localizations.answer_hint_200: The lighthouse light goes on and off 3 times consecutively, then twice. Take card 32.
localizations.machine_14: Impressed by your performance, the Fakir keeps his promise and takes you to the entrance of the sacred temple. The man who could assist you is inside that temple.
localizations.machine_21: Once your passport is visaed, you leave the consulate and go to the Suez Canal to continue your adventure.
localizations.machine_34: After sending your telegram, you board the next ship that will take you to the American continent.
localizations.machine_48: After walking for a few minutes through dense vegetation, you find your guide securing shelter for the night. The next day, you reach Calcutta, elephant-riding. Once there, your guide finds your next means of transportation.
localizations.machine_300: You spot Fix in the New York crowd and force him to let the ship leave for London.
localizations.machine_77_step_1: Once the watch is set, you board your train to Suez.
localizations.machine_48_broken: Good Lord! This compass is broken! There must be another compass somewhere around…
localizations.machine_77_step_1_hint: Spot your next destination to have a better idea of what time it is.
localizations.machine_77_step_1_answer: Thanks to your diary, you know that Suez is your next destination. The train leaves at 8:45 pm, in 5 minutes according to Monsieur Fogg. So, it is 8:40 pm. Take this opportunity to set your watch to 8:40.
localizations.machine_fix_text1: He suspects Mr. Fogg of being the one who robbed the Bank of England. This vile character will do anything to slow you down during your journey. Be careful and spot him in time to prevent him from interfering with your plans.
localizations.machine_fix_text2: Enter the card numbers you see him in.
localizations.machine_14_instructions2:
To cross this plank on all fours, each player (up to 4 players max.) must participate by using at least one finger.

When all the players are ready, press the OK button.
localizations.machine_14_intro1: A fakir who was carefully listening at that moment stops you and offers his help.
localizations.machine_14_intro2_1: Do you want to go to Calcutta? I know someone who could assist you…
localizations.machine_14_intro2_2: If you manage to cross this plank on all fours, I will take you to that person.
localizations.machine_15_text: Pushed by the winds for days, you reach the Hong-Kong coast.
localizations.machine_300_not_ready1: You must find Detective Fix and force him to lift his edict. But it appears you're not yet a shrewd observer.
localizations.machine_300_not_ready2: Press the dedicated button and enter at least five card numbers on which you see him.
localizations.machine_300_ready1: You must find Detective Fix and force him to lift his edict. You did it great until now.
localizations.machine_300_ready2: He is hiding in the crowd. Force him to come out of hiding and press Fix.
localizations.machine_34_desc: Briefly press to produce a dot, and press at length to produce a dash.
localizations.machine_77_end: Indeed, going eastward, you had gone towards the sun, and the days therefore diminished for you as many times 4 minutes as you crossed degrees in this direction, i.e.; 24 hours for a full circle. Going eastward to travel around the world, you saw the sun pass the meridian 81 times while your friends from the Reform Club in London only saw it pass the meridian 80 times. So, you win your bet: Travel the world in 80 days.
localizations.machine_85_text:
This is not the right number.
The hot-air balloon cannot take off. Try something else.
